privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated vehicle data using Multiecuscan's built-in simulation mode. The target vehicle (Alfa Romeo MiTo 1.4 TB petrol 2010) and the customer work order are entirely fabricated via PowerShell setup scripts. The task.json contains no explicit metadata claims to verify. The evaluation script (verifier.py) strictly requires the final report to mention at least 3 readiness monitors (including explicit mentions of the Catalyst, EVAP, and O2/lambda sensors), document any DTCs, provide a readiness verdict (READY, NOT READY, or CONDITIONAL), suggest drive cycle guidance, and explicitly reference UK MOT regulations. A minor discrepancy was observed where the work order specifies engine code 955A8.000, but the provided reference CSV only lists 955A7.000 for the MiTo.
